label: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 19

extraction.one_line_summary: CORT Magnus won from a 7-rider break with perfect CRADDOCK leadout timing in final 800m, holding peloton by 18 seconds after DSM/BikeExchange coordination failure.
tactical_patterns: OLIVEIRA Rui attacked early to shed perceived passengers from 18-rider break, forcing quality selection down to 7.
BikeExchange and DSM failed to coordinate chase until too late, allowing break margin despite having sprinters Matthews/Dainese.
SIMMONS Quinn forced tempo knowing sprinters advantage, but CRADDOCK leadout for CORT proved decisive under 800m.
Break delayed final sprint until inside 3km using cat-and-mouse, exploiting GC teams safety-riding near finish.

rider_signals.CORT Magnus: positive: Third stage win after team lost three riders including GC leader CARTHY shows exceptional opportunism in weakened squad.
rider_signals.MEINTJES Louis: negative: Crash from peloton compression eliminated top-10 GC hopes with two stages remaining, costly positioning error.
rider_signals.CRADDOCK Lawson: positive: Leadout work for CORT in break situation demonstrates tactical flexibility beyond domestique role.
rider_signals.OLIVEIRA Rui: neutral: Sprinter attacking to thin break field shows awareness of limitations but ultimately couldn't match CORT timing.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: 2:30 margin over MAS with time trial remaining suggests conservative safety riding in finale was correct.
